segments.text:
And the boy Samuel ministered to the LORD before Eli.
Now in those days the word of the LORD was rare and visions were scarce.
And at that time Eli, whose eyesight had grown so dim that he could not see, was lying in his room.
Before the lamp of God had gone out, Samuel was lying down in the temple of the LORD, where the ark of God was located.
Then the LORD called to Samuel, and he answered,
Here I am.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: He ran to Eli and said,
Here I am, for you have called me.
segments.speaker: He ran to Eli and

segments.text: I did not call,
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: Eli replied.
Go back and lie down.
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: So he went and lay down.
Once again the LORD called,
Samuel!
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: So Samuel got up, went to Eli, and said,
Here I am, for you have called me.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: My son, I did not call,
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: Eli replied.
Go back and lie down.
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: Now Samuel did not yet know the LORD, because the word of the LORD had not yet been revealed to him.
Once again, for the third time, the LORD called to Samuel. He got up, went to Eli, and said,
Here I am, for you have called me.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Then Eli realized that it was the LORD who was calling the boy.
Go and lie down,
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: he said to Samuel,
and if He calls you, say, ‘Speak, LORD, for Your servant is listening.’
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: So Samuel went and lay down in his place.
Then the LORD came and stood there, calling as before,
Samuel! Samuel!
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: And Samuel answered,
Speak, for Your servant is listening.
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: Then the LORD said to Samuel,
I am about to do something in Israel at which the ears of all who hear it will tingle.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: On that day I will carry out against Eli everything I have spoken about his family, from beginning to end.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I told him that I would judge his house forever for the iniquity of which he knows, because his sons blasphemed God and he did not restrain them.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Therefore I have sworn to the house of Eli, ‘The iniquity of Eli’s house shall never be atoned for by sacrifice or offering.’
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Samuel lay down until the morning; then he opened the doors of the house of the LORD. He was afraid to tell Eli the vision,
but Eli called to him and said,
Samuel, my son.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Here I am,
segments.speaker: Samuel

segments.text: answered Samuel.
What was the message He gave you?
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: Eli asked.
Do not hide it from me. May God punish you, and ever so severely, if you hide from me anything He said to you.
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: So Samuel told him everything and did not hide a thing from him.
He is the LORD,
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: replied Eli.
Let Him do what is good in His eyes.
segments.speaker: Eli

segments.text: And Samuel grew, and the LORD was with him, and He let none of Samuel’s words fall to the ground.
So all Israel from Dan to Beersheba knew that Samuel was confirmed as a prophet of the LORD.
And the LORD continued to appear at Shiloh, because there He revealed Himself to Samuel by His word.
